methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
44.01 is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $37M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA) operating in the Climate & Energy sector. Key private peers include Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy, TAE Technologies, and Redwood Materials, representing a substantive cohort for benchmarking.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, which uses total capital raised as a proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields $74M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against three same-stage, same-sector companies: Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M. The median of this cohort is $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This method is directly applicable given the availability of contemporaneous comparables.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a valuation range of $63M (low) to $172M (high), with a median estimate of $112M. The substantial spread reflects two sources of uncertainty: the disparity between stage-progression methodology, which anchors to capital deployed rather than market multiples, and peer-median methodology, which captures meaningful variation in how market participants value similar-stage companies in this nascent sector. Revenue multiples and unit economics were not available to refine this range further.
